title: Anti-abuse threat model + guardrails (anti-botnet, consent, signed client, never-trust-one-worker, kill switch)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. A distributed donated-compute system must not become a botnet: this threat model documents the consent-first, never-trust-one-worker posture, DoS/poisoned-unit handling, and the kill switch, mapping each mitigation to an implemented control. It depends on worker-012.
objective: Document the anti-abuse threat model and guardrails (anti-botnet, consent, signed client, never-trust-one-worker, kill switch) with each mitigation mapped to an implemented control.
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the anti-botnet posture (consent-first, no covert/non-consenting use, no coordinator-executed code), the never-trust-one-worker rule, DoS/poisoned-unit handling, and the kill switch; each mitigation maps to an implemented control.
output: A document recording the anti-abuse threat model and guardrails mapped to implemented controls.
